role_blueprints.title: AI Engineer
role_blueprints.summary: Builds AI product features and intelligent user experiences that ship to production.
responsibilities: Turn AI ideas into real features

state_requirements.why_it_matters: Production AI systems depend on strong coding and debugging skills.
Math helps reason about model behavior and trade-offs.
Model training and evaluation are central to the role.
Modern AI products rely on neural and transformer systems.
Visual analysis helps explain model behavior and product impact.

role_blueprints.title: ML Engineer
role_blueprints.summary: Designs, trains, validates, and operationalizes machine learning systems.

state_requirements.why_it_matters: ML workflows are code-heavy and experimental.
Strong statistics and optimization drive robust models.
This is the core skill domain for the role.
Modern ML often extends into neural systems.
Production ML must fit real application architectures.

role_blueprints.title: Data Scientist
role_blueprints.summary: Uses analysis, experimentation, and predictive modeling to drive better decisions.

state_requirements.why_it_matters: Python supports analysis, modeling, and data preparation.
Inference and experimentation are central to the role.
Predictive modeling supports many data science tasks.
Insights must become clear stories and decisions.
Most real analyses begin with database fluency.

role_blueprints.title: Data Engineer
role_blueprints.summary: Builds the pipelines, data platforms, and reliable movement of information across systems.

state_requirements.why_it_matters: Automation and data transforms are implemented in code.
Data engineers design and optimize database layers.
Pipeline orchestration and quality are at the heart of the role.
Modern pipelines often run on cloud infrastructure.
Reliable delivery keeps data systems safe and repeatable.

role_blueprints.title: Prompt Engineer
role_blueprints.summary: Designs prompt systems, evaluation loops, and AI workflows that feel reliable and useful.

state_requirements.why_it_matters: Prompt design is the signature skill for the role.
Model behavior is easier to shape when you understand the underlying systems.
Prompt flows often depend on external tools and APIs.
Visualization helps compare prompt quality and evaluation results.
Prompt systems are usually embedded in application code.

role_blueprints.title: Cloud Architect
role_blueprints.summary: Designs scalable, secure, and cost-aware cloud systems for complex products.

state_requirements.why_it_matters: Platform capability knowledge is the base of architecture design.
Connectivity and exposure are architecture concerns.
Architects often design for containerized, orchestrated systems.
Delivery flow affects maintainability and reliability.
Architecture decisions depend on system design depth.

role_blueprints.title: Cybersecurity Specialist
role_blueprints.summary: Protects applications, infrastructure, and delivery flows from modern threats.

state_requirements.why_it_matters: Traffic paths and exposure are core security concerns.
Threat models and application security are core to the role.
Security teams must understand the infrastructure they protect.
Modern security work includes automated policy and pipeline checks.
Security must be designed into architecture decisions.

role_blueprints.title: Software Developer
role_blueprints.summary: Builds maintainable software features, services, and release-ready product logic.

state_requirements.why_it_matters: Solid coding fundamentals power all product work.
Many product features depend on service-side logic.
Applications depend on good data modeling and queries.
Reliable releases keep development fast and safe.
Design awareness helps features scale cleanly.

role_blueprints.title: Full Stack Engineer
role_blueprints.summary: Designs and ships complete product experiences across frontend, backend, and integration layers.

state_requirements.why_it_matters: The role spans rich, polished interfaces.
Full-stack work must include strong service-side engineering.
Apps depend on well-structured persistent data.
Modern products connect multiple external and internal services.
Full-stack engineers benefit from strong end-to-end architecture thinking.

role_blueprints.title: Blockchain Developer
role_blueprints.summary: Builds blockchain products, smart contract flows, and Web3-connected application logic.

state_requirements.why_it_matters: Programming fluency supports backend and tooling work around chains.
Many blockchain applications still rely on strong service-side logic.
This is the signature technical domain for the role.
Security failures are especially costly in blockchain systems.
Distributed product decisions require strong systems thinking.
